answer:
The Otter.ai API uses Bearer token authentication for all requests [1][2]. Depending on your specific integration, authentication is handled in one of two ways:

For direct API use, generate an API key via the Developer tab in your Otter.ai account settings [1]. Include this key in the Authorization header of your HTTP requests:

```http
Authorization: Bearer YOUR_API_KEY
```

For Enterprise accounts requiring OAuth 2.0, you must obtain client credentials from your account manager [3]. You then exchange these credentials for an access token via the OAuth token endpoint and provide that token in the Authorization header for subsequent requests [3]. Note that access tokens obtained via OAuth expire and require a refresh strategy [3].
